Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 232 [Temple-building rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] x x [x aš₂]-ri-ša₂ iš-x-BU-⸢ma⸣

(o 1) [...] ... [...] ... her places and [...] ... it (a door) fell and has broken.

o 22

[...] x ⸢ŠUB⸣ut-ma ḪAŠir

o 33

[...] tukum-bi itudapin-duḫ-a-ta ul i-še-eṭ

(o 3) [...] he does not neglect (the series) 'If from (the month) Apinduha'.

o 44

[...] x x-ma ⸢GIBIL⸣eš e-pe-ši

(o 4) [...] ... and making (the door) anew [...] ... he visits her places assiduously and [...] ... [...] ... you set up a libation vessel. You set out 1 offering table for Kusu.

o 55

[...] x x aš₂-ri-ša₂ ⸢iš⸣-te-ne₂-ʾe-e-ma

o 66

[x x] x [x x] x ⸢dug⸣A.⸢DA.GUR₅⸣ GUBan 1 GI.DU₈ ana dKU₃.SU₃ tara-kas₂

o 77

udu⸢SISKUR₂⸣ [x] ⸢BARA₂?.GA? KAŠ⸣ SAG GA BALqi₂-ma

(o 7) You present an offering, filtered [...], first-class beer (and) milk, and you overlay that door ....

o 88

gišIG BI x x x ⸢tu⸣-lab-ba-aš₂

o 99

⸢3⸣ GI.DU₈-MEŠ [x] ⸢DINGIR⸣ E₂ d⸢INANA E₂⸣ u ⸢DINGIR.MAḪ⸣ tara-kas₂

(o 9) You set out 3 offering tables [...] the god of the temple, the goddess of the temple and Belet-ili.

o 1010

3 udu⸢SISKUR₂⸣ [BAL]qi₂ 2 ⸢GI.DU₈⸣-MEŠ ana ⸢IGI⸣ gišIG ana dPAP.SUKKAL

(o 10) You present 3 offerings. You set out 2 offering tables in front of the door for Papsukkal and NinGANdu. You present 2 offerings. You light a pile of brushwood.

o 1111

⸢u₃⸣ dNIN.⸢GAN⸣.[DU₇] ⸢tara⸣-kas₂ 2 ⸢udu⸣SISKUR₂ BALqi₂ ab-ra MU₂aḫ

o 1212

x [x] x ŠUB[di?] x KU ⸢GI⸣-MEŠ ina UGU LA₂aṣ

(o 12) You lay down ... [...] .... You stretch out a ... of reeds on top.

o 1313

[...] ⸢NIG₂?⸣ ina ⸢UGU? GAR⸣an A-MEŠ GUBan [tug₂]⸢šid⸣-du GID₂.DAad

(o 13) You put [...] ... on top. You set up water. You draw the curtain.

o 1414

[x a-še]-⸢er⸣-ra ⸢ER₂?⸣ [x] x er₂-⸢ra?⸣ [x] x x ⸢ER₂⸣.ŠEM₃.MA

(o 14) The taqribtu-lament (is) '[...] in lament'. The eršemmakku-lament (is) '[...] ... in tears [...] ...'.

o 1515

⸢ER₂.ŠA₃.ḪUN⸣.GA₂ ana ⸢DINGIR E₂⸣ u dPAP.SUKKAL ta-na-aš₂-ši

(o 15) You raise an eršahungû-lament to the god of the temple and Papsukkal.

o 1616

[x še?]-⸢ri₃?⸣ ina UR₃ ⸢KEŠ₂? tara⸣-kas₂ KI SAR A-MEŠ KU₃ SU₃

(o 16) [...] the morning you set out a ritual assemblage on the roof. You sweep the ground. You sprinkle pure water.

o 1717

[x] ⸢1⸣en ER₂.ŠA₃.[ḪUN].⸢GA₂⸣ ana dUTU tara-kas₂

(o 17) You set out [...] one eršahungû-lament to Šamaš.

o 1818

ina ⸢UGU?⸣ [x] ŠUB⸢di?⸣ [x] ⸢KU?⸣ GI-MEŠ ina ⸢UGU⸣ LA₂aṣ

(o 18) You lay down [...] on top. You stretch out a [...] ... of reeds on top.

o 1919

KU? x [... GAR]an A-MEŠ GUBan tug₂[šid]-du ⸢GID₂⸣.DAad

(o 19) You put ... [...]. You set up water. You draw the curtain.

o 2020

umun [...] ⸢ki⸣-a ⸢ER₂⸣ dilmunki niŋin-⸢na ER₂⸣.ŠEM₃.MA

(o 20) The taqribtu-lament (is) 'Lord, [...] on [...] (and) earth'. The eršemmakku-lament (is) 'Dignitary, return!'.

o 2121

[... ER₂].ŠA₃.ḪUN.GA₂ x [x] x UD x [x] ⸢giš⸣IG BI SIG₃-ma

(o 21) [...] an eršahungû-lament ... [...] ... [...]. You smash that door and [...] ... door [...] ... [...] ... [...] ... Ninildum and Kusibanda [...] ... [...] ... that door is made anew.

o 2222

[...] x gišIG [x] ⸢a?-na?⸣ x [x x] ME? A

o 2323

SAR [x] x x [d]⸢NIN.ILDUM₂⸣ u d⸢KU₃.SI₂₂⸣.BAN₃.DA

o 2424

[x] x GUR ⸢BU⸣ [x] x x gišIG BI GIBILeš in-ne₂-ep-pu-uš

o 2525

[e-nu]-⸢ma⸣ gišIG [x x] ⸢GIBIL⸣eš ina DU₃-⸢ka⸣ ina ITI ⸢šal⸣-[me]

(o 25) When a door [...], on your making (it) anew, in a favourable month [...] propitious [...] you set up [...]. [...] ... [...] ... the god of the temple, [...] you present [...] offerings.

o 2626

[x x ŠE].GA [...] ⸢GUB⸣an [...] x x

o 2727

[...].AM₃ DINGIR E₂

o 2828

[... udu]⸢SISKUR₂⸣ BALqi₂

o 2929

[...] ⸢GI⸣.DU₈-MEŠ ina IGI gišIG

(o 29) [...] you set out [...] offering tables in front of the door [...] Papsukkal and NinGANdu. You present 2 offerings.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[x dPAP].SUKKAL u dNIN.GAN.DU₇ ⸢tara-kas₂⸣ 2 uduSISKUR₂ BALqi₂

r 22

⸢ab⸣-ra [x] dNUSKA MU₂[aḫ] ⸢udu⸣SILA₄ ina UGU ta-kas₃-si

(r 2) You light a pile of brushwood [...] Nusku. You bind a lamb on top.

r 33

[x x] GUBan tug₂šid-du GID₂.[DAad] ⸢e₂⸣ tur₃-gin₇ niŋin-na-am₃ ER₂

(r 3) You set up [...]. You draw the curtain. The taqribtu-lament (is) 'The temple is enclosed like an animal stall'.

r 44

⸢de₃⸣-em-mar de₃-em-⸢mar⸣ ina ŠA₃ i-za-mur

(r 4) In between he sings 'Let me place, let me place'.

r 55

⸢ušum⸣ gud₃ nud-[a] ER₂.ŠEM₃.MA ER₂.ŠA₃.ḪUN.GA₂

(r 5) The eršemmakku-lament (is) 'Snake lying in the nest'.

r 66

ana ⸢DINGIR⸣ E₂ u dPAP.SUKKAL ta-⸢na?⸣-[aš₂?]-⸢ši? IGI? NIR₃⸣ [x x] GAL

(r 6) You raise an eršahungû-lament to the god of the temple and Papsukkal. You thread [...] a silver string an eye-shaped bead (made) of chalcedony (and) an [...] (made) of great [...]. You bow down to Nusku and Papsukkal.

r 77

[x] ⸢GU⸣ KU₃.BABBAR E₃ak ana dNUSKA u ⸢d⸣PAP.SUKKAL [tuš?]-ken₂

r 88

NA₄-MEŠ šu-nu-ti ina SAG gišIG ta-al-lal

(r 8) You hang those stone beads on the top of the door.

r 99

⸢NIG₂?⸣.DIM₂.DIM₂.MA an-na-a-ti te-ep-pu-uš-ma

(r 9) You perform these actions and the god will be at peace with the king, the temple, the land (and) the city. The (portended) evil of that (damaged) door will not approach the king.

r 1010

DINGIR KI LUGAL E₂ KUR IRI SILIM⸢im⸣ ḪUL gišIG BI  ; ana LUGAL NU TE

(colophon)
r 1111

ki-i pi-i imGID₂.DA GABA.RI ⸢TIN⸣.TIRki ša₂ m⸢MU?⸣—ḪE₂.GAL₂šu₂

(r 11) (Written) in accordance with the wording of a single-columned tablet, a copy of Babylon belonging to Šumu-libšu and Nabu-eṭir-napšati, the son of Harriru, the [...] of Marduk.

r 1212

u₃ mdMUATI—KARir—ZI-MEŠ A mḫar-⸢ri⸣-ri [x] ⸢d⸣AMAR.UTU
